book_claims.mechanism: Ewe's milk has a different fatty acid profile than cow's milk, with higher omega-3 content and conjugated linoleic acid (CLA)
book_claims.text: Pecorino is consumed by Sardinian centenarians and described as a high omega-3 cheese made from ewe's milk

supplementary_research.finding: Sheep's milk fat contains 2–3× more CLA than cow's milk; CLA associated with anti-inflammatory and anti-carcinogenic effects in animal and some human studies
Sardinian centenarian dietary patterns include regular sheep's milk cheese consumption; region-specific dairy-longevity association noted in multiple epidemiological studies

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Higher than cow's milk cheese; ruminant-derived CLA associated with anti-inflammatory effects
Well-absorbed; hard cheeses provide concentrated calcium without lactose intolerance issues for most people
Complete protein; aged cheeses have lower lactose than fresh dairy

synergies.note: Both goat/sheep milk cheeses preferred over cow's milk cheese in the Longevity Diet
Traditional Italian pairing; use modest portions per Longevity Diet principles
Classic Sardinian blue-zone combination; legume protein + sheep cheese fat and protein
